title: Reorganize String
problem_statement: Rearrange a string so that no two adjacent characters are the same, or return an empty string if impossible.

examples.explanation: Reorganizing yields a valid string with no equal adjacent letters.

constraints: s consists of lowercase English letters.
intuition: Use a max-heap to always place the most frequent remaining character next, while avoiding immediate repeats.

approaches.name: Greedy heap reconstruction
approaches.description: Count characters, then repeatedly append the two most frequent unused chars while storing the previous char for future use.
approaches.why_not: Attempting to place characters without frequency consideration can fail on highly skewed counts.

interview_qa.q: Why store the previous character separately?
interview_qa.a: It prevents immediately using the same character twice while still allowing it back when safe.
When any character appears more than half of the string length rounded up.
